scope: Navigation and page rendering for Trading / Live Trading nav items. Categories: Contract Violations, Logic Gaps, Dead Code, Integration Gaps.

findings.title: Minimal Mistakes masthead.html does NOT support children dropdown navigation (ROOT CAUSE)
findings.claim: Commit 91ed4e1 states: Navigation updated: Trading is now a dropdown (Backtest Results + Live Trading)
findings.reality: Minimal Mistakes v4.28.0 masthead.html has no children/dropdown support. It renders link.url for each item; when url is nil (because children was used instead), href resolves to empty string, making the link do nothing. Sub-items are never rendered.
findings.impact: ROOT CAUSE of the reported bug. Trading link rendered as href='' (does nothing), Live Trading was completely invisible in navigation.

findings.title: Broken navigation deployed to production without pre-deploy validation
findings.claim: Navigation changes should be tested before deploying to production
findings.reality: Broken children YAML in commit 91ed4e1 was pushed directly to main and deployed via GitHub Pages. Jekyll build succeeded because nil href is not a build error. No local preview or CI validation caught the issue.
findings.impact: Broken navigation was live in production. No safeguards exist against future configuration mistakes.

findings.title: Greedy navigation hides Trading/Live Trading on medium viewports
findings.claim: All 6 navigation items should be accessible to users
findings.reality: Minimal Mistakes greedy-nav plugin removes items from the end of the visible list on narrow viewports. With 6 items, Trading and Live Trading (positions 4-5) get pushed to hamburger menu on medium viewports (768-1100px).
findings.impact: Trading nav items hidden behind hamburger menu on tablet viewports. Users may not notice the hamburger toggle.

findings.title: No cross-link between Trading and Live Trading pages
findings.claim: Related trading pages should cross-reference each other (originally intended as dropdown sub-items)
findings.reality: Neither page contains any link to the other. The only way to navigate between them is via the top masthead navigation.
findings.impact: Users landing on one trading page via direct link have no indication the other exists.

findings.title: Chart.js CDN dependency with no fallback or SRI
findings.claim: Pages with charts should render reliably and external scripts should be integrity-verified
findings.reality: Both pages load Chart.js from jsDelivr CDN without integrity or crossorigin attributes. If CDN is blocked, charts render as blank canvases. No SRI means CDN compromise could inject malicious JavaScript.
findings.impact: Chart sections blank on restricted networks. CDN compromise risk.

findings.title: No guard against navigation regression
findings.claim: Navigation configuration should prevent the children mistake from recurring
findings.reality: No comments warn against children in masthead nav. No CI validates navigation.yml structure. MM docs for sidebar nav actively document children syntax, creating confusion.
findings.impact: Latent regression risk. The same mistake was already made once.

findings.title: layout: none in index.html should be layout: null
findings.claim: Jekyll specifies layout: null to skip layout processing
findings.reality: layout: none is the string 'none', not YAML null. Jekyll looks for none.html layout, fails to find it, and silently falls back to no layout. Works by accident.
findings.impact: Build warning. Future Jekyll versions could change missing-layout behavior.

findings.title: GitHub Pages reports custom_404: false despite custom 404.md existing
findings.claim: Custom 404 page should be served for non-existent URLs
findings.reality: GitHub Pages API shows custom_404: false. This likely reflects UI configuration, not Jekyll-generated 404.html behavior.
findings.impact: Likely no actual impact. Jekyll 404.html convention is respected regardless.
